content.thinking:
The user wants me to continue working on the HQ project. According to the harness protocol, I need to:

1. READ .harness/progress.txt → session history
2. READ .harness/features.json → next feature (passes:false)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature at a time
4. TEST visually (bun tauri dev)
5. COMMIT after feature passes
6. UPDATE .harness/progress.txt

Let me start by reading the harness state files to understand where we are.

file.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
# Session Progress Log

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
- Installed dependencies:
  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup

## Key decisions:
- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
- State management: @zazcart/tools
- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components

================================================================================

file.content:
import {
  Brand,
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Text,
  TextContent,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { useState } from 'react';

function App() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const header = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Brand src="" alt="HQ">
            <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
          </Brand>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page header={header}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <TextContent>
              <Text>Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Text>
              <Text>Click count: {count}</Text>
            </TextContent>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}>
              Increment
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Kanban Board</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <TextContent>
              <Text>BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE</Text>
            </TextContent>
            <Label color="gold">Coming Soon</Label>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>
    </Page>
  );
}

export default App;

[package]
name = "hq"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard"
authors = ["you"]
edition = "2021"

[lib]
name = "hq_lib"
crate-type = ["lib", "cdylib", "staticlib"]

[build-dependencies]
tauri-build = { version = "2", features = [] }

[dependencies]
tauri = { version = "2", features = [] }
tauri-plugin-shell = "2"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
serde_json = "1"

[profile.release]
strip = true
lto = true
codegen-units = 1
panic = "abort"

Based on the search results, here's the key information about migrating Text/TextContent to Content in PatternFly 6:

## PatternFly 6 Text → Content Migration

### Key Change
Text: Renamed to "content".(no content)While some changes are handled by codemods, some changes could require manual intervention and should be kept in mind as you upgrade.

### The New Content Component
The `<Content>` component allows you to establish a block of HTML content and apply simple, built-in styling. `<Content>` can be used for any element supported by the component property (including h1 through h6, hr, p, a, small, blockquote, and pre).(no content)You cannot nest other components within `<Content>`, and doing so can cause styling overrides or other conflicts. Instead, you can use the `<Content>` component's properties to achieve the same results. For example, to create a level 1 heading, you should pass component="h1" to `<Content>`, instead of nesting a `<Title>` component within `<Content>`.(no content)Similarly, when you need to add a divider to a page, you should utilize the hr property of `<Content>` (which is styled as a divider), rather than using a separate `<Divider>` component. HTML elements wrapped by `<Content>` are styled by the content component.

### New Features in PatternFly 6
Added guidance for editorial styling to our content component (formerly called text content).(no content)Editorial styling increases the font size of body text and small text by 1 tier, where body text becomes "lg" and small text becomes "md". To apply editorial styling, use `isEditorial`.(no content)Added `<hr>` element support to the content component.

### Migration Example
In PatternFly 6, instead of importing `Text` and `TextContent`, you now import `Content` from `@patternfly/react-core`:

```jsx
// PatternFly 6
import { Content } from '@patternfly/react-core';

<Content component="h1">Title</Content>
<Content component="p">Body text</Content>
```


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's information about the PatternFly 6 React Page component and Masthead:

## Page Component with Masthead in PatternFly

A page component defines the basic layout of a page, with either vertical or horizontal navigation.

### Masthead Structure

Mastheads contain the `<MastheadMain>` that wraps a `<PageToggleButton>` and `<MastheadBrand>`. The `<MastheadBrand>` wraps `<MastheadLogo>`. The masthead also contains the page's header toolbar within `<MastheadContent>`.(no content)The `<MastheadMain>` component includes the smaller area that typically contains the `<MastheadToggle>` and a `<MastheadLogo>`. `<MastheadContent>` represents the main portion of the masthead, and will typically contain a `<Toolbar>` or other menu-like components, like a `<Dropdown>`.

### Integrating Masthead with Page

A `<Page>` typically has a masthead prop that often contains a masthead component.(no content)To add horizontal navigation to the top of a `<Page>`, add the navigation inside of a `<ToolbarItem>` in the `<Toolbar>` that is passed to the `<MastheadContent>` of the `<Masthead>`.

### Key Features

When the `isManagedSidebar` property is true, it manages the sidebar open/close state, removing the need to pass both `isSidebarOpen` into the `<PageSidebar>` and `onSidebarToggle` into the `<PageToggleButton>`.

### MastheadLogo Customization

In addition to being able to pass a string to the component property of MastheadLogo, you can provide more fine-tuned customization by passing a callback that returns a component. Router links can be used for in-app linking in React environments to prevent page reloading.

### Page Section Filling

By default, page sections will not expand to fill in the available vertical space in a page. To change this default behavior, pass `isContentFilled` to the `<Page>` component and `isFilled={true}` to any child `<PageSection>` or `<PageGroup>` component that you want to fill the space.

Note: The search results reference PatternFly 6 documentation (indicated by the `--pf-v6-c-page` CSS variable prefix seen in the results).


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.prompt: Extract the Page component API and props. What prop is used for the header/masthead? Show usage examples.

content.content:
# Page Component API and Masthead Props

## Masthead Prop

The **`masthead`** prop is used to pass the header component to a `<Page>`. According to the documentation:

> "Masthead component (e.g. <Masthead />)"

## Usage Examples

### Basic Structure
The masthead typically contains:
- `<MastheadMain>` — smaller area with toggle and logo
- `<MastheadContent>` — main portion with toolbar/menus
- `<MastheadToggle>` — contains `<PageToggleButton>`
- `<MastheadLogo>` — contains `<MastheadBrand>`

### Implementation Pattern
```jsx
<Page
  masthead={
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadToggle>
          <PageToggleButton />
        </MastheadToggle>
        <MastheadLogo>
          <MastheadBrand>Logo</MastheadBrand>
        </MastheadLogo>
      </MastheadMain>
      <MastheadContent>
        {/* Toolbar or menu components */}
      </MastheadContent>
    </Masthead>
  }
  sidebar={/* ... */}
>
  {/* Page sections */}
</Page>
```

## Related Navigation Props

- **`isManagedSidebar`** — manages sidebar open/close automatically
- **`onPageResize`** — callback for resize events

input.old_string:
import {
  Brand,
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Text,
  TextContent,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { useState } from 'react';

function App() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const header = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Brand src="" alt="HQ">
            <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
          </Brand>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page header={header}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <TextContent>
              <Text>Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Text>
              <Text>Click count: {count}</Text>
            </TextContent>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}>
              Increment
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>

import {
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Content,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { useState } from 'react';

function App() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const masthead = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page masthead={masthead}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Content>
            <Content component="p">Click count: {count}</Content>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}>
              Increment
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Kanban Board</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE</Content>
            <Label color="yellow">Coming Soon</Label>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>
    </Page>
  );
}

import {
  Brand,
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Text,
  TextContent,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { useState } from 'react';

function App() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const header = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Brand src="" alt="HQ">
            <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
          </Brand>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page header={header}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <TextContent>
              <Text>Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Text>
              <Text>Click count: {count}</Text>
            </TextContent>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}>
              Increment
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Kanban Board</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <TextContent>
              <Text>BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE</Text>
            </TextContent>
            <Label color="gold">Coming Soon</Label>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>
    </Page>
  );
}

export default App;

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - F010 Complete + PatternFly 6 API Fix
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues in App.tsx:
  - Changed Text/TextContent to Content component
  - Changed Page header prop to masthead prop
  - Fixed Label color "gold" to "yellow"
- Created app icons using `bun tauri icon` command
- Verified TypeScript compiles without errors
- Verified Tauri app builds and runs successfully
- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP

## Key learnings:
- PatternFly 6 renamed Text/TextContent to Content
- Page component uses masthead prop, not header
- Label colors: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orangered, grey, yellow

## F010: PASSED ✅
- Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6 running
- Icons generated from SVG source

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

================================================================================

================================================================================

## What was done:
- Fixed PatternFly 6 API issues in App.tsx:
  - Changed Text/TextContent to Content component
  - Changed Page header prop to masthead prop
  - Fixed Label color "gold" to "yellow"
- Created app icons using `bun tauri icon` command
- Verified TypeScript compiles without errors
- Verified Tauri app builds and runs successfully
- Visual testing passed with Playwright MCP

## Key learnings:
- PatternFly 6 renamed Text/TextContent to Content
- Page component uses masthead prop, not header
- Label colors: blue, teal, green, orange, purple, red, orangered, grey, yellow

## F010: PASSED ✅
- Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6 running
- Icons generated from SVG source

- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
# Session Progress Log

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
- Installed dependencies:
  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup

## Key decisions:
- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
- State management: @zazcart/tools
- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
- PatternFly has LogViewer, Chat, and Terminal components

================================================================================

file.content:
# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Vision

**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.

## Important Distinction: Three Separate Concepts

Understanding HQ requires separating three distinct concepts:

| Concept | What It Is | Example |
|---------|------------|---------|
| **Harness (method)** | A pattern for AI agents to work on long tasks across sessions via state files | `.harness/progress.txt` + `features.json` |
| **HQ in development** | This project is being BUILT using the Harness method | The `.harness/` folder in THIS repo |
| **HQ as product** | Once built, HQ will MANAGE other projects that use Harness | Visual dashboard for external projects |

**Key insight:** The `.harness/` folder here is for building HQ itself. When HQ is complete, it will open and visualize `.harness/` folders from OTHER projects.

---

## Core Philosophy

> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"

HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
- **Multi-project management** via tabs
- **Harness state visualization**

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
│                    (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6)                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       State Management Layer                         │
│                  (@zazcart/tools state-machine)                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
│  │ (portable-pty)  │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     External Processes                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer                | Technology          | Version | Purpose                              |
|----------------------|---------------------|---------|--------------------------------------|
| **Desktop Runtime**  | Tauri               | 2.x     | Native app shell, Rust backend       |
| **Frontend**         | React               | 19.x    | UI framework                         |
| **UI Components**    | PatternFly          | 6.x     | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/tools      | 0.1.0   | State machine + schema validation    |
| **PTY Emulation**    | portable-pty (Rust) | latest  | Process spawning and control         |
| **File Watching**    | notify (Rust)       | latest  | Real-time harness file changes       |
| **IPC**              | Tauri Commands      | -       | Frontend ↔ Backend communication     |

### Why PatternFly 6?

PatternFly provides ready-made components for our use cases:
- **Chat/AI components** - For agent communication UI
- **Log viewer** - Real-time log display with ANSI support
- **Terminal** - Embedded terminal views
- **Tabs** - Multi-project navigation
- **Cards/Panels** - Kanban board layout
- **Dark theme** - Built-in dark mode support

---

## Feature Specifications

### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management

**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.

**UI Elements:**
- Tab bar at top (PatternFly Tabs)
- New tab button (+)
- Close tab button (X)
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)

**Behavior:**
- Each tab represents a project directory
- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
- Active tab is highlighted
- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop

### F002: Kanban Board View

**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.

**Columns:**
```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

**Card Structure:**
- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
- Title
- Description (truncated)
- Status badge
- Priority indicator
- Click to expand details

**Data Source:**
- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
- Status mapped to columns:
  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
  - `passes: true` → DONE

### F003: Real-Time Log Panel

**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)

**Features:**
- PatternFly LogViewer component
- ANSI color support
- Timestamp prefix
- Process filter
- Search within logs
- Clear button
- Auto-scroll toggle

**Technical:**
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)

### F004: PTY Process Controller

**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.

**Capabilities:**
- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
- Resize PTY on window resize
- Kill process
- Restart process

**Rust Implementation:**
```rust
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
    Ok(pair)
}
```

### F005: Harness State Visualization

**Description:** Display current harness state visually.

**Components:**
- Progress summary card
  - Features completed / total
  - Current session info
  - Last commit
- Active feature highlight
- Session timeline (from progress.txt)

### F006: Project Selector Dialog

**Description:** Open new project from file system.

**UI:**
- PatternFly Modal
- Recent projects list

content.thinking: Agora tenho os exemplos de como usar @zazcart/tools. Vou ler mais do HQ_SPEC.md para ver a API completa.

Agora entendo a API do @zazcart/tools:

1. `createModel` - Para criar schemas de validação
2. `createSlice` - Para criar slices síncronos com reducers
3. `createAsyncSlice` - Para criar slices assíncronos
4. `createMachine` - Para combinar slices em uma state machine
5. `useMachine` - Hook para acessar state e actions
6. `useSelector` - Hook para derivar estado

Vou criar o store.ts no src/state/ com a estrutura definida.

content.thinking: Os erros são porque o @zazcart/tools está inferindo tipos de forma diferente do esperado. O problema é que a biblioteca não está inferindo corretamente os tipos das propriedades do initial state.Vou tentar uma abordagem diferente - criar uma tipagem mais explícita ou usar um tipo mais específico para o state. Alternativamente, posso desabilitar strict TypeScript temporariamente ou usar type assertions.Por enquanto, vou simplificar bastante o store para garantir que funcione e depois refinamos. Vou criar uma versão mais simples que usa tipos explícitos com type assertions onde necessário.

content.text: A biblioteca está tendo problemas de inferência de tipos. Vou simplificar o store para funcionar:

lines: -// TYPES (manually defined for better TypeScript support)
-// UI Slice - tab management, theme, layout
-    update: (state, payload: { id: string; updates: Partial<Project> }) => {
+    update: (state: ProjectsState, payload: { id: string; updates: Partial<Project> }) => {
-    updateStatus: (state, payload: { id: string; status: Process['status'] }) => {
+    updateStatus: (state: ProcessesState, payload: { id: string; status: Process['status'] }) => {
-    updatePid: (state, payload: { id: string; pid: number }) => {
+    updatePid: (state: ProcessesState, payload: { id: string; pid: number }) => {
-// Logs Slice - process logs with ring buffer
-      // Ring buffer: keep last MAX_LOGS_PER_PROCESS entries
-// Features Slice - synchronous for now, will be async when Tauri backend is ready
-    updateFeature: (state, payload: { id: string; updates: Partial<Feature> }) => {
+    updateFeature: (state: FeaturesState, payload: { id: string; updates: Partial<Feature> }) => {
return { total, completed, remaining: total - completed };
+export type { UIState, ProjectsState, ProcessesState, LogsState, FeaturesState };

import {
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Content,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { useState } from 'react';

function App() {
  const [count, setCount] = useState(0);

  const masthead = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page masthead={masthead}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Content>
            <Content component="p">Click count: {count}</Content>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => setCount((c) => c + 1)}>
              Increment
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Kanban Board</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE</Content>
            <Label color="yellow">Coming Soon</Label>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>
    </Page>
  );
}

export default App;

content.thinking: Vou atualizar o App.tsx para usar o state machine. Por enquanto, vou manter a estrutura simples mas demonstrar o uso do appMachine.

input.new_string:
import {
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Content,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { appMachine, selectors } from './state';

function App() {
  // Use state machine hooks
  const { state: ui, actions: uiActions } = appMachine.useMachine('ui');
  const { state: features } = appMachine.useMachine('features');

  // Derived state via selectors
  const stats = selectors.featureStats({ ui, features, projects: {}, processes: {}, logs: { entries: {}, autoScroll: true, filter: '' } });

  const masthead = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page masthead={masthead}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Content>
            <Content component="p">Theme: {ui.theme}</Content>
            <Content component="p">Projects open: {ui.projectOrder.length}</Content>
            <Content component="p">Features: {stats.completed}/{stats.total} complete</Content>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => uiActions.setTheme(ui.theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')}>
              Toggle Theme
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Kanban Board</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE</Content>
            <Label color="yellow">Coming Soon</Label>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>
    </Page>
  );
}

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import {
  Button,
  Card,
  CardBody,
  CardTitle,
  Content,
  Label,
  Masthead,
  MastheadBrand,
  MastheadMain,
  Page,
  PageSection,
  Title,
} from '@patternfly/react-core';
import { useState } from 'react';
// State machine is configured in ./state/store.ts
// TODO: Integrate with components once @zazcart/tools hook issues are resolved

function App() {
  // Temporary local state until state machine integration is complete
  const [theme, setTheme] = useState<'light' | 'dark' | 'system'>('system');
  const [projectCount] = useState(0);
  const stats = { completed: 1, total: 10 }; // F010 is done

  const masthead = (
    <Masthead>
      <MastheadMain>
        <MastheadBrand>
          <Title headingLevel="h1" size="xl">HQ</Title>
        </MastheadBrand>
        <Label color="blue" isCompact>Harness Orchestrator</Label>
      </MastheadMain>
    </Masthead>
  );

  return (
    <Page masthead={masthead}>
      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Welcome to HQ</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">Your dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions.</Content>
            <Content component="p">Theme: {ui.theme}</Content>
            <Content component="p">Projects open: {ui.projectOrder.length}</Content>
            <Content component="p">Features: {stats.completed}/{stats.total} complete</Content>
            <Button variant="primary" onClick={() => uiActions.setTheme(ui.theme === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark')}>
              Toggle Theme
            </Button>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>

      <PageSection>
        <Card>
          <CardTitle>Kanban Board</CardTitle>
          <CardBody>
            <Content component="p">BACKLOG | TODO | DOING | REVIEW | QA | DONE</Content>
            <Label color="yellow">Coming Soon</Label>
          </CardBody>
        </Card>
      </PageSection>
    </Page>
  );
}

export default App;

message.content: Parece que você ficou esperando o processo terminar, mas ele não vai terminar, é um servidor rodando, não?

content.thinking:
O usuário está correto! Quando rodei `bun tauri dev`, ele inicia um servidor que fica rodando indefinidamente. Eu fiquei esperando com `sleep 30 && tail...` mas o processo nunca termina porque é um servidor.

A abordagem correta é:
1. Rodar o servidor em background com `run_in_background: true`
2. NÃO esperar ele terminar - apenas verificar se está rodando
3. Usar o Playwright para testar a UI enquanto o servidor roda

Preciso ajustar minha abordagem para não ficar "travado" esperando servidores.

content.text:
Exatamente! Você tem razão. O `bun tauri dev` e `bun run dev` são servidores que ficam rodando indefinidamente. Eu deveria:

1. **Rodar com `run_in_background: true`** ✅ (fiz isso)
2. **NÃO esperar terminar** - apenas dar um `sleep` curto para o servidor subir
3. **Testar com Playwright** enquanto roda
4. **Matar depois** com `kill` ou `lsof -ti :5173 | xargs kill`

O erro foi usar `sleep 30 && tail...` esperando o output final - o que nunca vai acontecer porque é um servidor.

A abordagem correta seria:

```bash
# Iniciar em background
bun tauri dev  # com run_in_background: true

# Esperar servidor subir (5-10 segundos)
sleep 10

# Verificar se está rodando
curl -s http://localhost:5173 > /dev/null && echo "Server up!"

# Testar com Playwright
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot

# Quando terminar, matar o servidor
lsof -ti :5173 | xargs kill
```

Vou lembrar disso nas próximas sessões. Quer continuar com **F001** agora?

message.content: Você não vai lembrar, salve no harness

content.thinking: O usuário está absolutamente certo! Eu não vou lembrar nas próximas sessões porque cada sessão começa sem memória. Preciso salvar essa lição no harness (progress.txt ou CLAUDE.md) para que futuras sessões tenham acesso a essa informação.

## Next steps:
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management with PatternFly Tabs

## LESSONS LEARNED (READ THIS!)

### Dev Servers (bun tauri dev, bun run dev)
NEVER wait for dev servers to "finish" - they run indefinitely!

Correct approach:
```bash
# 1. Start in background
bun tauri dev  # with run_in_background: true

# 2. Wait briefly for server to start (5-10 sec)
sleep 10

# 3. Verify server is up
curl -s http://localhost:5173 > /dev/null && echo "Server up!"

# 4. Test with Playwright MCP
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot

# 5. Kill server when done
lsof -ti :5173 | xargs kill
```

WRONG: `sleep 30 && tail output` expecting it to finish

================================================================================

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
# Session Progress Log

================================================================================
SESSION: 2026-01-02 - Initial Setup
================================================================================

## What was done:
- Created project structure at ~/antonio/HQ
- Wrote HQ_SPEC.md with full system specification
- Configured .claude hooks (session-start, protect-deps, quality-check)
- Set up .harness/ with progress.txt and features.json
- Initialized Tauri 2.x project with React 19
- Installed dependencies:
  - react@19.2.3, react-dom@19.2.3
  - @patternfly/react-core@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-icons@6.4.0
  - @patternfly/react-styles@6.4.0
  - @zazcart/tools@0.1.0
  - @tauri-apps/cli@2.9.6, @tauri-apps/api@2.9.1
- Created basic App.tsx with PatternFly components
- Created Rust backend (src-tauri/) with Tauri 2.x setup

## Key decisions:
- Stack: Tauri 2.x + React 19 + PatternFly 6
- State management: @zazcart/tools
- PTY: Rust portable-pty crate (to be added)
- File watching: Rust notify crate (to be added)

## Next steps:
- F009: Configure @zazcart/tools state machine
- F010: Verify Tauri app runs (bun tauri dev)
- F001: Implement multi-project tab management

## Notes:
- PatternFly 6 supports React 17, 18, and 19
